page: Amazon Web Services r5b.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core and memory performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score is 40671.85, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 11027.0, both falling within the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only large block speed of 107.9 GB/sec and a write speed of 47.88 GB/sec. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1380.89 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression rate of 23.57 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5b.12xlarge offers a high-density memory configuration suitable for memory-bound workloads without the added cost of local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs. However, the lack of local storage requires relying on network-attached storage, and the weaker single-core performance represents a tradeoff for applications requiring high single-threaded throughput. This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive applications such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides 8.0 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, alongside weaker single-core execution. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations and in-memory caching rather than compute-heavy or single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
